lint.command: make lint (golangci-lint run --timeout 5m on milestone packages)

blockingIssues.description: BUG: applyLectureFilters returns filter counts (int, int) but does NOT return the filtered client.Lectures slice. In the original code, `selected = filterEmptyLectures(selected)` and `selected = filterNoAudioLectures(selected)` reassigned the slice in-scope. After extraction to a separate function, the local `lectures` parameter reassignment has no effect on the caller's `selected`. Result: empty and noaudio lecture filtering is silently disabled in interactive mode — user filter preferences are ignored. Fix: have applyLectureFilters return (client.Lectures, int, int) instead of (int, int), and use the returned slice in the caller.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Change applyLectureFilters signature to return the filtered Lectures slice along with the counts. Update the caller to use the returned filtered slice.

nonBlockingIssues.description: MAKE_TARGETS variable is assigned but never used (dead code, pre-existing)

respondWithSuccess() omits 'error' field, creating schema inconsistency with respondWithEnvelope() which now includes 'error': null. Both are success-response helpers but produce different JSON shapes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The go-backend-worker skill should include a refactoring-specific verification step: when extracting helper functions, verify that all mutations of the original in-scope variables are preserved by either (a) returning the modified values, (b) using pointer receivers, or (c) writing a targeted test for the extracted function's contract. The current skill only says 'run existing tests' which missed this behavioral regression because interactive mode has no automated test coverage.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-lint-errors extracted applyLectureFilters from runInteractive, losing the mutation of the `selected` slice. Existing tests passed but the behavioral regression was not caught because interactive mode has no automated tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md could be more specific about HOW to verify behavior preservation during refactoring
rejectedObservations.reason: This is better addressed as a skill-level improvement (see suggestedGuidanceUpdates) rather than adding implementation details to AGENTS.md

rejectedObservations.observation: Server and CLI envelope patterns evolved independently without shared schema
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a known architectural observation, not actionable guidance for workers
